steps.title: Overview: Browser Workbench
steps.description: The preview tour showed renderer classes. This tour covers the imperative browser shell that owns controls, design switching, preview mode switching, URL encoding, export button state, purchase lists, and dialogs.

steps.title: Shell Markup
steps.description: The browser shell is created directly in TypeScript. The markup defines the main manufacturing tabs, preview pane, controls pane, and purchase list mount point.

steps.title: Control Sync
steps.description: Control sync pushes current settings and workbench state back into DOM inputs. It also refreshes design-specific controls, export controls, and readiness messaging.

steps.title: Preview Render Loop
steps.description: Preview rendering synchronizes workbench state, builds a fresh layout, and dispatches to the active preview renderer. This is the workbench side of the renderer contract.

steps.title: Input Event Handler
steps.description: The shared input handler updates state, remembers design-specific settings, synchronizes URL state, and rerenders. It keeps most controls on the same update path.

steps.title: Design Selection
steps.description: Design selection uses the settings memory module, switches to enclosure preview when needed, and moves the controls tab to design for static references.

steps.title: Design-Specific Controls
steps.description: The workbench chooses which specialized controls are visible based on fabrication method and active print design. That keeps Corsi and donut controls out of unrelated workflows.

steps.title: URL Sync
steps.description: URL sync writes both purifier settings and workbench state into query parameters. Shared URLs therefore preserve design, preview mode, fabrication method, and print volume.

steps.title: Workbench Styling
steps.description: CSS defines the dense workbench layout, split preview/settings areas, tabs, buttons, forms, and responsive constraints. The visual language lives in one stylesheet.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: What state is encoded into shared URLs?
answers: Both purifier settings and workbench state
Only the current Three.js camera matrix
steps.explanation: `syncUrl` starts at line 1565 in `src/app/main.ts` and appends values from both `encodeSettings` and `encodeWorkbenchState`.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: The browser workbench coordinates data flow: read controls, update state, create layout, update preview, refresh diagnostics, and encode URLs. The next tour follows the user-facing outputs attached to that state.
